articles.title_en: ‘All we could do was watch our merchandise burn’: Russian small business owners respond to losing their stock when Ukrainian drones hit two Wildberries warehouses
articles.lead_en: On the night of July 18, Ukrainian drones struck two Wildberries warehouses — one outside Moscow and one in the Tambov region. At least eight people were killed and more than 80 injured.
articles.title_raw: ‘All we could do was watch our merchandise burn’: Russian small business owners respond to losing their stock when Ukrainian drones hit two Wildberries warehouses
articles.lead_raw: On the night of July 18, Ukrainian drones struck two Wildberries warehouses — one outside Moscow and one in the Tambov region. At least eight people were killed and more than 80 injured.

articles.title_en: They pried the gates open with their hands. They jumped from a freight door. Workers describe the chaos inside two burning warehouses run by Wildberries, Russia’s largest online retailer.
articles.lead_en: Wildberries warehouses in Moscow and Tambov oblasts caught fire after Ukrainian drone strikes, and the evacuation of employees was marred by violations, according to eyewitness accounts cited by Post- magazine and the independent investigative outlet iStories. In some sections, managers kept workers inside the burning buildings; employees had to open automatic gates by hand, evacuate chaotically through narrow doorways, and flee the warehouse grounds through a forest.
